title: Verify that the --read-only-port argument is set to 0

input_row.title: Verify that the --read-only-port argument is set to 0
input_row.rationale: The Kubelet process provides a read-only API in addition to the main Kubelet API. Unauthenticated access is provided to this read-only API which could possibly retrieve potentially sensitive information about the cluster.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet Verify that the --read-only-port argument exists and is set to 0 . If the --read-only-port argument is not present, check that there is a Kubelet config file specified by --config . Check that if there is a readOnlyPort entry in the file, it is set to 0 .
input_row.remediation: If using a Kubelet config file, edit the file to set readOnlyPort to 0 . If using command line arguments, edit the kubelet service file /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf on each worker node and set the below parameter in KUBELET_SYSTEM_PODS_ARGS variable. --read-only-port=0 Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart kubelet.service
input_row.impact: Removal of the read-only port will require that any service which made use of it will need to be re-configured to use the main Kubelet API.
input_row.default_value: By default, --read-only-port is set to 10255/TCP . However, if a config file is specified by --config the default value for readOnlyPort is 0.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be evaluated using Kubernetes API or kubectl commands to check the Kubelet configuration.
- The audit mentions using command line arguments to verify the `--read-only-port` setting, which can be checked programmatically.
- The rationale indicates that this is a standard Kubernetes service property, which supports automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_read_only_port_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the following command to check the Kubelet configuration:
  ```bash
  ps -ef | grep kubelet
  ```
  This command will list the running Kubelet processes along with their command-line arguments.
  
- If the `--read-only-port` argument is present, verify that it is set to `0`. If it is not present, check the Kubelet configuration file specified by `--config` to ensure that the `readOnlyPort` entry is set to `0`.

- To check the Kubelet configuration file, you can use:
  ```bash
  kubectl get configmap kubelet-config -n kube-system -o yaml
  ```
  This command retrieves the Kubelet configuration from the ConfigMap, if it exists.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - Pass: The `--read-only-port` argument is set to `0` or the `readOnlyPort` entry in the Kubelet config file is set to `0`.
  - Fail: The `--read-only-port` argument is set to any value other than `0`, or the `readOnlyPort` entry is not set to `0` in the Kubelet config file.

- The expected secure configuration is that the Kubelet should not expose a read-only port, thus preventing unauthenticated access to sensitive information.
